title: Peripheral Problems
drDigitalIntro: Peripherals are devices you connect to your computer — printers, external monitors, mice, USB drives, and headphones. When a peripheral isn't working: unplug and replug it. Try a different USB port. Restart your computer with the peripheral connected. Check System Settings > Printers & Scanners (for printers). Update drivers if needed. Make sure the peripheral is charged if it's wireless. Most peripheral problems resolve with these simple steps.

playgroundTask.question: You plug in a USB flash drive but your computer doesn't recognize it. What should you try first?

options.text: Buy a new computer — your USB ports are broken
Unplug it and try a different USB port on your computer

drDigitalSuccess: A different USB port often solves recognition issues — one port may be faulty while others work fine.
drDigitalHint: What's the simplest, least drastic step when a USB port doesn't recognize something?
